takeaways: Senior housing enters 2026 with strong tailwinds as private capital returns — PERE reports the sector's steep post-COVID discounts are vanishing as institutional investors reenter.
CRE capital markets are thawing: Colliers forecasts 15–20% sales volume growth in 2026 with the 10-Year Treasury stabilized at 4.00–4.25% and commercial rates in the 4.99–8.75% range.
Agentic AI is moving from pilot to production in proptech, with operators prioritizing ROI-focused workflow automation over 'AI-washed' point tools.

items.headline: Senior Living Discounts Vanish as Private Capital Returns in 2026
items.summary: PERE reports that lingering post-COVID trepidation from private real estate investors toward US senior living is fading in 2026, and with it the steep discounts the sector had offered. Demand from older adults continues to rise while development pipelines remain thin by historical standards — a favorable supply-demand setup for operators like Lloyd Jones.

items.headline: Senior Living Faces 'Choose Your Own Adventure' Moment in 2026
items.summary: Senior Housing News reports the sector is entering 2026 with wind at its back, but operators face strategic inflection points around capital allocation, wage pressure, and service model differentiation. Demand continues to outpace thin development pipelines, creating pricing power for well-positioned communities.

items.headline: Kisco Plans Las Vegas IL/AL/Memory Care Community in Summerlin
items.summary: Kisco Senior Living announced plans for a desert contemporary community in Summerlin, Las Vegas offering independent living, assisted living and memory care, with some of the largest residences in the market. The project underscores continued operator confidence in Sunbelt growth markets.

items.headline: Seniors Priced Out of Solo Living as Roommate Share Triples
items.summary: NPR reports the share of adults 65+ seeking to rent with a roommate has tripled over the past decade as housing costs squeeze older Americans. The trend reinforces the affordability gap and the opportunity for middle-market senior housing products.

items.headline: CRE Debt Capital Liquidity Set to Grow as Fed Turns Dovish
items.summary: CRE Daily reports stabilizing inflation near 3% and a more flexible Fed stance are paving the way for stronger CRE debt capital liquidity in 2026, improving access to financing for acquisitions and refinancings. A key positive for sponsors with near-term debt maturities.

items.headline: Commercial Loan Rates Range 4.99%–8.75% as 10-Year Stabilizes
items.summary: As of April 9, 2026, conventional commercial mortgage rates sit between roughly 4.99% and 8.75% depending on property type and borrower credit, with the 10-Year Treasury holding in the 4.00–4.25% band since mid-2025. Rate stability is enabling underwriters to price deals with more confidence.

items.headline: Colliers: CRE Sales Volume to Rise 15–20% in 2026
items.summary: Pricing has found a floor and deal velocity is rising, with Colliers forecasting a 15–20% increase in CRE sales volume in 2026 as institutional and cross-border capital reenter the market. Deloitte's outlook echoes the cautious optimism across sectors.

items.headline: Newmark Sees Sector Divergence in 2026 CRE Outlook
items.summary: Newmark's sector-by-sector 2026 outlook highlights diverging performance across CRE, with residential (including senior housing) and industrial outperforming, while office and some retail segments continue to reprice. Capital is selectively reentering the strongest fundamentals.

items.headline: Agentic AI Reshapes Proptech Workflows in 2026
items.summary: ICSC reports agentic AI is emerging as the next phase of proptech, automating procurement, pricing, inventory and event programming for retail and real estate operators. For senior housing, this points to near-term opportunities in automated vendor management, resident services scheduling, and predictive maintenance.

items.headline: 'Workflow' Becomes Proptech's Buzziest Buzzword
items.summary: Commercial Observer reports that agentic AI is stitching together data platforms and property management tools to form the long-missing workflow layer in CRE tech. Owners are moving past point-solution fatigue toward integrated platforms that deliver measurable operational ROI.

items.headline: Operators Raise the Bar on AI Vetting and ROI
items.summary: ICSC finds real estate companies are increasingly separating 'AI-washed' tools from meaningful solutions with built-in AI, demanding measurable ROI, performance improvements and operational efficiency before rollout. Rigorous pilot frameworks are becoming standard.

items.headline: AI Moves from Experimentation to Delivery Across Built Environment
items.summary: Estate Agent Today reports that small-scale AI pilots and proofs of concept are now translating into deployed solutions in 2026, with AI becoming a practical driver of efficiency, cost reduction, and better decision-making across property operations.

items.headline: Executive Turnover Slows but AI Strategy Clarity Lags
items.summary: Journal of Accountancy reports only 19% of 2,500+ global executives saw >50% leadership turnover in the past year, down from 43% a year ago. Yet lack of strategic clarity and ineffective decision-making remain the top two barriers to leadership effectiveness for the second straight year.

items.headline: Microsoft: Leaders Anchor AI to Outcomes, Not Tools
items.summary: Microsoft reports leaders pulling ahead in 2026 are taking a differentiated approach — anchoring AI to measurable business outcomes rather than tool adoption — and are scaling deployments securely, responsibly and repeatably across the enterprise.

items.headline: DDI: 'Quiet Cracking' and Burnout Reshape 2026 Leadership Agenda
items.summary: DDI's 2026 trends highlight human + AI leadership, flatter org structures, rising burnout and 'quiet cracking,' and job-hugging-stalled mobility as defining pressures. Emotional intelligence is the through-line for leaders navigating lean teams.

items.headline: Learning Speed Beats Strategy Polish in 2026
items.summary: Barry O'Reilly argues the best organizations in 2026 won't be those with the most polished strategies but the ones that learn fastest when those strategies break — a counterintuitive call for adaptive operating rhythms over annual planning rituals.

items.headline: Wellhub: 89% of Employees Link Wellbeing to Higher Performance
items.summary: Wellhub's State of Work-Life-Wellness 2026 finds 89% of employees say they perform better when prioritizing wellbeing, and reports a decisive shift away from 'work hard, play hard' toward lives built around wellness. A signal for senior housing operators managing frontline workforces.

items.headline: 'Emotional Fitness' Emerges as the New Wellness Category
items.summary: Dr. Axe highlights 2026 as the year emotional fitness becomes a standalone wellness category, combining cognitive performance and emotional resilience, powered by AI analytics, at-home biomarker tests, and advanced wearables beyond step and sleep tracking.

items.headline: WebMD: Workplace Wellness Becomes a 2026 Strategic Priority
items.summary: WebMD Health Services reports 2026 is the year to elevate workplace wellbeing from cost center to strategic priority, with programs tied to engagement, loyalty, trust and productivity metrics rather than participation alone.

items.headline: Global Wellness Summit Names 10 Trends Shaping 2026
items.summary: The Global Wellness Institute released its 10 wellness trends for 2026, spotlighting personalization, prevention, and real-life well-being over aspirational aesthetics. Senior living communities can leverage these trends in programming and marketing.
